check.title: Check Suspicious Interaction

dropzone.dragText: Drag and drop your file here, or click to browse

textarea.placeholder: Paste the text of the chat, message, or transcript here (e.g., 'This is the Cyber Crime Branch. Do not disconnect...')

results.timelineDesc: This visual timeline illustrates the detected phases of the scam. Click on any active phase to expand its explanation.
results.evidenceDesc: Key sentences extracted from the interaction indicating potential fraud indicators.
results.complaintDesc: An automatically drafted complaint document prepared for submission to law enforcement.
results.helplineText: Official Cyber Crime Helpline: Call 1930 immediately to report financial fraud.

alert.criticalDesc: This interaction matches known patterns of a Digital Arrest scam. Take immediate defensive action.

familyModal.success: Alert sent successfully! Your contact has been notified.

about.title: Understanding Digital Arrest Scams
about.subtitle: Equip yourself with the knowledge to identify and defeat public safety scams.
about.scamIntro: A 'Digital Arrest' scam is a highly sophisticated form of coercion where fraudsters impersonate law enforcement officers, cyber police, custom authorities, or CBI agents. They contact victims via video calls, claiming the victim's identity has been linked to illegal contraband, money laundering, or high-profile crime. They threaten immediate arrest unless the victim complies with a 'digital isolation' video call and transfers money for verification.

phases.title: The Five Phases of Digital Arrest
phases.authority: 1. Authority Impersonation: Fraudsters wear uniforms or present fake IDs, posing as senior police or federal officers.
phases.fear: 2. Fear & Coercion: The victim is threatened with severe legal action, public humiliation, or immediate jail time.
phases.isolation: 3. Isolation: The victim is ordered not to close the video call, tell family, or contact any lawyer.
phases.money: 4. Money Demand: The victim is instructed to transfer funds to a 'government safe verification account' immediately.
phases.control: 5. Constant Control: Continuous video surveillance is maintained to ensure the victim has no window to seek help.

facts.title: Crucial Facts to Remember
facts.fact1: Government agencies and law enforcement NEVER issue arrests via video calls.
facts.fact2: There is no legal provision for a 'Digital Arrest' or virtual custody in Indian law.
facts.fact3: Official agencies will never ask you to transfer funds to bank accounts for verification.

footer.text: PRAHARI. Created for the AI Digital Public Safety Challenge. Protecting digital sovereignty.
